name: ANZAC Day
traditions: Dawn services at war memorials; marches of veterans and descendants; playing The Last Post; two-up gambling; Anzac biscuits.
historicalBackground: ANZAC Day marks April 25, 1915, when Australian and New Zealand Army Corps (ANZAC) landed at Gallipoli. It evolved into a day of remembrance for all who served.
description: Australia and New Zealand's solemn day of remembrance, honoring the ANZAC tradition with dawn services and veterans' marches.
